title: Bacterial Cryopreservation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4rggu","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) for additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Take a dense culture and transfer a volume of cells (usually 20 mL) into a sterile centrifuge tube
Spin down the culture at 4,500 rpm, 10 min 00:10:00
Resuspend pellet into 2 mL of the appropriate growth medium
Split the 2 mL into two cryogenic vials by adding 1 mL to each vial
Remove cryogenic vials one at a time from the freezer
Using a sterile pipette tip, scrape a small amount of frozen cells and place into 20 mL sterile growth medium. Slowly move the cells to the appopriate growth conditions. undetermined
Place cryogenic vial immediately back into the freezer
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
